div1.entryFree:
Prorogue, 1) to delay: death --d, Rom. II, 2, 78. nothing may p. it, IV, 1, 48.
2) to draw out, to linger out, to keep in a languishing state: that sleep and feeding may p. his honour even till a Lethe'd dullness, Ant. II, 1, 26. nor taken sustenance but to p. his grief, Per. V, 1, 26.
